purpose: Convince a mixed public-health team that pathogen genomics is worth considering, while avoiding technology hype.
readerActionAfterReading: Define the public-health decisions that genomic information could improve before choosing tools or infrastructure.
mustCover: Value comes from changed public-health action, not data production alone.
Genomics depends on sampling, epidemiology, metadata, laboratory quality, reporting routes, and response capacity.
Economic claims must be setting-specific and should not imply universal savings.
doNotClaim: Do not say genomics is always cost-saving.
Do not imply sequencing alone detects all outbreaks or replaces epidemiology.
Do not generalize high-income implementation results without context.
conditionalVariantsNeeded: Director/funder version should emphasize decision value, scenarios, and economic uncertainty.
Technical readers can see more detail on workflow and data dependencies.
figuresTablesWanted: One simple value-chain figure linking sequence data to interpreted public-health action.
knownGaps: Need structured extraction of economic evaluation comparators and numeric estimates.
reviewNotes: Keep this section publication-like. Avoid noisy inline caveats; put weaker evidence needs in Needs more work.

purpose: Make readers treat infrastructure as a set of responsibilities and service dependencies, not a purchase list.
readerActionAfterReading: Name who owns workflows, compute, storage, identity, validation, monitoring, support, and failure recovery.
mustCover: Cloud/local/HPC/managed platform choices are responsibility models.
Service implementation includes LIMS, validation, accreditation, reporting, staff, and user engagement.
Constraints such as internet reliability, staff availability, IT support, and data residency should change recommendations.
doNotClaim: Do not say cloud is the default best answer.
Do not present laptop workflows as equivalent to validated routine service.
Do not hide support and maintenance responsibilities.
conditionalVariantsNeeded: No bioinformatics staff: raise managed platform, shared service, or external support text.
Data residency concern or cloud not allowed: lower cloud-first wording and raise governance review.
figuresTablesWanted: Responsibility model table for SaaS/PaaS/IaaS/local/managed service.
knownGaps: Need stronger source-backed procurement and support-model examples from public-health agencies.
reviewNotes: This is central to Gnomey. Keep practical and implementation-oriented.

purpose: Explain the complete operational lifecycle so teams do not optimize only sequencing or analysis.
readerActionAfterReading: Map sample receipt, metadata capture, sequence generation, QC, workflow execution, interpretation, reporting, sharing, storage, and retention.
mustCover: A service handles records and decisions, not just FASTQ files.
Sample and metadata linkage must survive handoffs.
Use cases change turnaround time, sharing, QC, and retention needs.
doNotClaim: Do not provide organism-specific QC thresholds until source-backed.
Do not imply one universal lifecycle for every pathogen programme.
conditionalVariantsNeeded: Enteric bacteria and foodborne programmes need repository/submission detail.
Respiratory virus programmes need variant/lineage monitoring and rapid sharing detail.
No LIMS should trigger extra identifier-lineage guidance.
knownGaps: Need formal extraction from repository metadata guidance and organism-specific QC sources.
reviewNotes: Use this as the main place to integrate figures and tables seamlessly.
